notes: Executed by the conformance parity runner against a local proxy harness with the captured Shopify payload supplied as the upstream read response; no live Shopify writes are performed. The catalog capture selects the rich collection metadata fields modeled by HAR-151 while preserving nested product pagination shape. The catalog's opaque pagination cursors and server-side query filtering (title-search case folding, SQL-datetime sort keys, collection_type / product_id predicates) encode Shopify-internal search-index state that the proxy cannot reconstruct from local store state, so the de-seeded proxy forwards the catalog read upstream and returns Shopify's response verbatim (it reads the real catalog rather than replaying a /__meta/seed snapshot). The search/sort/empty/membership targets assert per-alias slices of that single forwarded response — the former local-replay targets exercised the removed seed-catalog engine, which is behavior the de-seeded proxy structurally cannot reproduce, so they now verify the forwarded payload directly. Recorded live against harry-test-heelo (no /__meta/seed precondition). Publication-status filtering stays coordinated with the collection publication model; saved-search filters remain outside this comparison until saved search state exists.
